topic: Southern Distance Quadratic — post-queue_144 patch review
change_summary: Queue_144 pushed 2026-04-15T23:41 citing r_eq fix, Sydney/Santiago geometry rewrite, Karney dedup, verdict text update, circular phrasing sharpened, boilerplate trimmed. detail_evidence 5268->5400 (+132), detail_verdict 952->293 (boilerplate stripped). V50.6 paragraph correctly patched to r_eq. Sydney/Santiago geometry rewrite lands. Karney dedup done. Verdict text now links to post-hoc standard.
our_argument_summary: The c4 patch-pass successfully landed five of seven c4 holes: r_eq replaces ellipse axis ratio in the V50.6 paragraph; Sydney/Santiago geometry rewrite removes opposing-rims error; Karney doubling consolidated to one clause; verdict text stripped of redundant boilerplate; QF27/QF28 nonstop hedged. Verdict Misleading holds. Two residual defects: (1) the same ellipse-axis-ratio error survives in a SECOND paragraph (Scale comparison) that was not patched, creating an internal self-contradiction within detail_evidence; (2) three different precision claims across four text layers (finding <0.5%, tldr sub-meter, detail/verdict sub-millimetre).

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: No change since c4: find_curve.py still a skeleton with 6 hardcoded cities and an empty body; no repo script performs the claimed 20-route regression. V50.6 H(r) parameters are static strings.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Hardcoded throughout. The 5.2% mean and 0.0% Oslo-Stockholm are static display strings.
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: The retreat-to-V50.6 escape remains closed (both stages evaluated). No new escape clause opens.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None. Structural claims (find_curve.py skeleton, no 20-route regression, post-hoc evolution note) remain accurate.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: No monitor.py domain for WIN-027. Quadratic coefficients, R-squared, and V50.6 performance numbers are all static in the dome site text.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: The quadratic distance distortion is the signature of the azimuthal equidistant projection. Standard cartography predicts exactly this shape; the dome labels it a discovery.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Evolution note in raw-text/02-wins.txt: v22 circular-disc failure on southern/cross-equator distances was patched by WIN-027 quadratic. Correction fitted AFTER the failure.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: No derivation from dome geometry to the quadratic coefficients. Values are regression outputs from an unreproducible pipeline.

holes_found.description: Internal self-contradiction in parameter enumeration within detail_evidence. The V50.6 paragraph (patched in this cycle) correctly names the four distance parameters as (H_0, lambda, EW scale 0.9941, r_eq = 14,105 km). Two paragraphs later, the Scale comparison paragraph still reads: "The dome needs a base disc radius, an exponential height function, an ellipse axis ratio, and a longitude scale factor." This is the SAME error c4 flagged (ellipse axis ratio instead of r_eq), but it survived in a second location. The result is that within the same WIN, two paragraphs enumerate different fourth parameters. A dome defender can now say: "Your own text contradicts itself about what our parameters are — one paragraph says equatorial ring radius, the next says ellipse axis ratio. You clearly do not understand our model." This is worse than the original c4 error because self-contradiction within a single WIN is more damaging than cross-WIN inconsistency.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace the Scale comparison parameter list to match the V50.6 list: "The dome needs four fitted parameters (exponential amplitude, scale length, EW angular scale, and equatorial ring radius) and still reports 5.2% mean error." If the intent is to count total geometric parameters across WIN-027/030 (including the ellipse shape parameters from WIN-030), state that explicitly: "The dome distance-and-shape stack requires at least six fitted parameters across WIN-027/030/057" rather than mixing distance parameters with shape parameters in a list of four.

holes_found.description: Three-layer precision inconsistency. The finding (summary table) says "globe great-circle achieves <0.5% error." The tldr_evidence says "sub-meter precision globally." The detail_evidence and detail_verdict_text say "sub-millimetre absolute accuracy." These are three different precision claims, each technically defensible in context (finding = relative error, tldr = practical survey accuracy, detail = algorithmic accuracy), but a reader who encounters all three will see incoherence. Sub-millimetre (algorithmic) vs sub-meter (practical) is a 1000x gap that a careful reader will notice.
holes_found.recommendation: Align to one consistent claim with appropriate qualification. The strongest honest claim is "sub-millimetre algorithmic accuracy" (per Karney 2013) which makes the dome 5.2% look worst. Use this in the finding: "R-sq 0.79 = 21% unexplained; spherical geodesy achieves sub-millimetre absolute accuracy globally with two parameters." In the TLDR, change "sub-meter" to "sub-millimetre" to match. If the distinction between algorithmic and practical accuracy matters, add a parenthetical once, not three different numbers.

holes_found.description: The Scale comparison paragraph counts "a base disc radius" as a free parameter alongside "an exponential height function" (which contains H_0 and lambda). If counting free parameters, the disc radius (20,015 km) is arguably not independently fitted — it is pi times Earth radius, fixed by the AE projection geometry. Meanwhile H(r) has TWO free parameters. The paragraph thus both over-counts (disc_radius as free) and under-counts (H(r) as one, not two). This is a different problem from the ellipse-vs-r_eq error: even after fixing the fourth item, the parameter enumeration in this paragraph would be conceptually muddled.
holes_found.recommendation: Rewrite to count actual free parameters consistently. E.g.: "The dome needs at least four free parameters (H_0 = 8,537 km, lambda = 8,619 km, EW angular scale 0.9941, and r_eq = 14,105 km) and still reports 5.2% mean error — compared to Vincenty/Karney with two."

stronger_arguments.description: The c4 stronger-argument about Polaris elevation being a globe coordinate (latitude = Polaris elevation only on a sphere) was not integrated. This remains the most underused argument in WIN-027: the dome cannot compute r(city) without latitude, and latitude is a spherical-Earth concept. Explicitly naming this parasitism would close a loop with WIN-058 (WGS-84 dependency) and Section 1.8 (coordinate collapse).

The Oslo-Stockholm 0.0% tautology argument from c4 was also not integrated. This remains potent: both cities are at nearly identical latitudes (59.3-59.9N), so once the EW scale factor is fitted, the distance between any near-equal-latitude pair is trivially correct. Calling 0.0% a "prediction" when the fit was tuned on exactly this geometry is circular.
sources: raw-text/05-model.txt (EW scale factor and V12 performance)

kernel_of_truth.description: The dome correctly identifies that projecting a round Earth onto a flat disc produces systematic latitude-dependent distance distortions with approximately quadratic character at mid-to-southern latitudes. This is real mathematics — the AE projection signature.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The distortion IS the fingerprint of spherical curvature projected onto a plane. Fitting a correction to it means the dome has rediscovered curvature and labelled that rediscovery a win. The quadratic correction exists because the Earth is round; claiming it as dome physics inverts the causation.

deeper_analysis:
The c4 patch-pass was largely successful. Five of seven holes were fixed cleanly, and the detail_verdict_text trim from 952 to 293 characters is a significant quality improvement — removing the boilerplate that mechanically restated the post-hoc case. The r_eq correction in the V50.6 paragraph, the Sydney/Santiago geometry rewrite, and the Karney consolidation all land well.

The surviving moderate hole is a partial-fix artifact: the decider patched the V50.6 paragraph to say r_eq but missed the Scale comparison paragraph, which still says "ellipse axis ratio." This creates a new problem (internal self-contradiction) while fixing the old one (incorrect parameter name). The fix is a single text replacement.

The precision inconsistency (finding <0.5%, tldr sub-meter, detail sub-millimetre) was noted in c4 as minor and was not addressed. With the boilerplate gone, the reader path from summary table -> tldr -> detail is more streamlined, making the inconsistency more visible. Worth aligning.

Overall trajectory: WIN-027 text quality has improved steadily across c1-c5 reviews. The remaining defects are narrow and fixable. Confidence 0.92 (up from c4 0.90).

citations_verified: Disc chord ~30,000 km — verified under two-zone mapping (r_syd ~ 16,330 km, r_san ~ 16,300 km, delta_theta 137.3 deg, chord ~ 30,400 km)
r_eq = 14,105 km — matches raw-text/05-model.txt and WIN-057
citations_failed: "ellipse axis ratio" as fourth distance parameter in Scale comparison paragraph — factually wrong; should be equatorial ring radius r_eq = 14,105 km (same error as c4 hole #1, surviving in second location)
citations_unchecked: QF27/QF28 current operating schedule — still schedule-dependent but now hedged in text

advocate_mode.best_defense: A prepared defender post-patch would focus on the surviving internal contradiction: "In one paragraph you say our fourth parameter is the equatorial ring radius. Two paragraphs later you say it is the ellipse axis ratio. Your own critique cannot keep our parameters straight. How can we trust your analysis of our model if you cannot describe it consistently?" Secondary line: "You claim sub-millimetre accuracy in one place, sub-meter in another, and less-than-0.5% in a third. Which is your claim? If you cannot state your own benchmark consistently, why should anyone trust your comparison?" Neither attack touches the verdict, but both undermine credibility effectively.
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Fix the ellipse-axis-ratio remnant in the Scale comparison paragraph (hole #1). Align the three precision claims to one consistent figure. After both fixes, the self-contradiction attack vanishes entirely and the precision attack becomes cosmetic. Defense_survives drops to 1.

cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: WIN-027 V50.6 paragraph now matches WIN-057 parameter list (both cite r_eq = 14,105 km). But WIN-027 Scale comparison paragraph still says ellipse axis ratio — so WIN-027 is internally inconsistent and, by extension, one paragraph still conflicts with WIN-057. Once the Scale comparison paragraph is fixed, WIN-027 and WIN-057 will be fully consistent. WIN-058 cross-link (WGS-84 dependency on Polaris-elevation = latitude) still absent from WIN-027 text.

claims_checked: Sydney-Santiago great-circle 11,338 km — haversine: 11,346 km (within rounding)
Disc chord ~30,000 km — computed 30,400 km under two-zone mapping (r_eq=14,105 km, disc_radius=20,015 km, Sydney at r~16,330 km, Santiago at r~16,300 km, separation ~138 deg)
Chord/globe ratio ~3x — computed 30,400/11,346 = 2.68x (text says "almost three times")
WGS84 parameters (a=6,378,137 m, f=1/298.257223563) — standard values correct
quantitative_verification.discrepancies: "Almost three times" is slightly generous — actual ratio is 2.68x. Not a factual error ("almost" provides the hedge) but a precision-minded reader might quibble. The underlying kill-shot (30,000 km disc distance vs 11,338 km globe distance, impossible at subsonic speed) holds regardless.
